# Rigyd

> Rigyd is an AI-native platform that converts raw 3D models, images, and text into physics-enabled, simulation-ready OpenUSD assets for robotics training.

Rigyd is built by artlabs (https://artlabs.ai), a member of the NVIDIA Inception Program.

## What Rigyd Does

Rigyd solves the 3D data bottleneck in robotics simulation. Most 3D assets lack the physical properties (mass, friction, collision geometry) that simulators need. Rigyd uses AI to automatically:

- Estimate physics properties: mass, friction, restitution, center of mass
- Generate collision meshes via convex decomposition
- Add semantic labels and material identification
- Output validated OpenUSD with USDPhysics schemas (PhysicsRigidBodyAPI, PhysicsMassAPI, PhysicsCollisionAPI)

## Input Formats

- .glb (glTF Binary)
- .fbx (Autodesk FBX)
- .obj (Wavefront OBJ)
- Images (coming soon)
- Text descriptions (coming soon)

## Compatible Simulators

- NVIDIA Isaac Sim (via OpenUSD)
- MuJoCo (Google DeepMind)
- Gazebo / ROS 2 (Open Robotics)
- Unreal Engine (Epic Games)

## Pricing

- Starter: $29/month (or $19/month billed annually) — 30 SimReady objects per month, priority support
- Enterprise: Custom pricing — API access, customized for your needs, exclusive access to new features

## Key Statistics

- Only ~1,000 SimReady assets exist today in NVIDIA's library; typical warehouses have 50,000+ SKUs
- 40% better real-world performance when training combines synthetic + real data with physics-accurate assets (Source: NVIDIA GR00T N1 Benchmark, 2025)
- 97% cost reduction in scene preparation vs manual workflows
- $370K saved per 1,000-object simulation project

## Three Tracks

1. **3D to SimReady** (Available now): Upload existing 3D models. Rigyd optimizes geometry, adds physics properties, collision meshes, semantic labels, and packages as OpenUSD. Bulk processing via API available for enterprise plans.
2. **Text/Image to SimReady** (Coming soon): Describe what you need or upload images. AI generates 3D geometry with physics properties.
3. **Worlds to SimReady** (Coming soon): Describe environments your robots need. AI generates diverse, physically accurate 3D scenes.

## How It Works

1. Upload: Drop any 3D file (.glb, .fbx, .obj)
2. AI Estimates Physics: Generates collision meshes, identifies materials, estimates mass, friction, center of mass
3. Download SimReady USD: Validated OpenUSD with USDPhysics schemas ready for deployment
